---
title: "job search recovery skills"
format: html
---

## Job Search Recovery Skills

Employment stress can increase urges if not structured well. A planned routine can turn job search into a stabilizing activity rather than a trigger.

Key principles:

- Keep a daily schedule.
- Break large tasks into short blocks.
- Practice for predictable stress points (rejection, uncertainty, waiting).
- Use support after difficult interactions.

Meaningful direction protects recovery. Stressful transitions or role demands can increase risk if goals are unclear or daily actions drift away from personal values.

Clarifying values and translating them into concrete steps improves focus, persistence, and self-respect. This creates a stronger foundation for long-term behavior change.

::: callout
### Instructions
Build a one-week job search plan that protects recovery.
:::

| Task | Day/Time | Duration | Support step after task |
|---|---|---|---|
| Resume update | | | |
| Applications | | | |
| Outreach/networking | | | |
| Interview practice | | | |
